hnote_item_addressed: Item 3 — Lightweight 4-row falsification module on Overview
routing_priority: high (per HNOTE-007 stated priority: Item 1 hero swap > Item 3 falsification module > Item 5 eclipse badge > Item 4 How-to-Evaluate preface > Item 2 audit). Items 1, 2, 5 delivered as EXP-255 (also pending curmudgeon). EXP-256 is the next-priority item.
routing_note: Substantive overview rework per HNOTE-007 explicit directive ("Substantive overview rework. Should route through curmudgeon for adversarial review (NOT auto-integrate)"). Curmudgeon should apply DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric.

alignment_to_round2_review.review_recommendation: Round-2 #3 / Round-1 R3 / Principle #3: persistent "What would change our verdict" overview module
alignment_to_round2_review.addressed_by_exp_256: YES — 4-row module placed below the verdict bar chart on the Overview tab. Each row: condition + one-line description + current status. fm-jump anchor links to the full eg-falsifiability prose for context.
alignment_to_round2_review.relationship_to_exp_190: EXP-190 (full Scoreboard with new schema) stays parked — three open curmudgeon issues (ISS-1205/1241/1242) and waiting on human-group review. EXP-256 ships INDEPENDENT of EXP-190; the lightweight module pulls existing Evaluation Guide content into a structured surface and does not depend on EXP-190 schema work. If/when EXP-190 lands, it can subsume this module.
alignment_to_round2_review.chunking_decision: Delivered as a single EXP (not bundled with EXP-255). Rationale: the falsification module is a spatially separable block below the bar chart; EXP-255 handles the hero composition above it. Different surfaces, different review focus, independent integration risk. HNOTE-007 explicitly allowed "1 to 5 EXPs" at analyst discretion.

hero_composition_summary: Overview reading order after EXP-255 + EXP-256 both land: (1) title-block; (2) thesis-hero with display-scale operator-authored sentence; (3) verdict bar chart (EXP-209); (4) falsifiability-module (EXP-256); (5) accuracy-math-section; (6) downloads + verdict legend + part 1 prose. Triptych composition: claim (thesis) → result (distribution) → standard (criteria).
audit_finding_summary: eg-falsifiability section in Evaluation Guide (docs/index.html:532) contains four conditions verbatim matching the operator-specified module rows. No invented conditions. Status values (0 / 69, 1 partial, 0 / 69, pending) are grounded in the existing prose for each condition. Per-condition mapping is documented in EXP file under audit_findings_eg_falsifiability.status_values_grounded.
prereq_check: EXP-255 (overview-hero-rework) was delivered 2026-04-26T02:10:46Z and is awaiting curmudgeon review. EXP-256 anchors are unaffected by EXP-255 (HTML anchor is the generateVerdictBarChart call line; CSS anchor is the .accuracy-math-section @media closing brace). Either integration order works.
test_coverage_note: EXP file lists 5 new test assertions for the integrated patch. The decider should run tests/run.sh after integration; if existing tests fail, investigate (likely positional assertion needing update for the new section between bar chart and scorecard/thesis-hero).
summary: EXP-256 adds a lightweight 4-row "What would change our verdict" module to the Overview tab, placed immediately below the verdict bar chart. The four rows are verbatim distillations of the existing eg-falsifiability conditions in the Evaluation Guide tab; status values are drawn from the same source prose. Pure additive insertion via two patch_segments (HTML + CSS), both with verified-unique anchors. Independent of the parked EXP-190 Scoreboard work. Integration order with EXP-255 (also pending curmudgeon) is flexible — anchors do not collide.
